title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s strongest trust-and-safety signal is methodological: new work focuses on auditing model behavior, improving reliability under distribution shift, and probing whether multimodal systems actually use the evidence they claim to use. The only notable non-paper update is OpenAI’s cyber-defense access program, which matters because it expands controlled deployment of specialized models into a high-risk security domain.

items.title: OpenAI expands Trusted Access for Cyber with GPT-5.4-Cyber and API grants
items.why_it_matters: It broadens controlled use of frontier models in cyber defense, a sensitive dual-use deployment area.

items.title: Robust conformal prediction for LLMs using internal representations
items.why_it_matters: It targets more reliable uncertainty estimates when deployment conditions differ from calibration data.

items.title: Do vision-language models truly perform vision reasoning? A rigorous study of the modality gap
items.why_it_matters: It tests whether VLMs rely on visual evidence or mostly exploit language-side shortcuts.

items.title: ASMR-Bench: Auditing for Sabotage in ML Research
items.why_it_matters: It benchmarks whether auditors can catch subtle model-enabled sabotage in automated research workflows.

items.title: Information Router for mitigating modality dominance in vision-language models
items.why_it_matters: It addresses a common failure mode where multimodal systems over-rely on one input channel.

items.title: ArtifactNet detects AI-generated music via forensic residual physics
items.why_it_matters: It adds provenance and detection tooling for synthetic media, relevant to misuse and authenticity checks.

items.title: Where do vision-language models fail? World-scale analysis for image geolocalization
items.why_it_matters: It maps multimodal failure patterns in a capability with privacy, surveillance, and misuse implications.

items.title: From benchmarking to reasoning: large-scale evaluation of LLMs on Vietnamese legal text
items.why_it_matters: It extends domain-specific evaluation for high-stakes legal use beyond surface benchmark scores.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
